test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_biology_material
test_cases.scenario: Generate flashcard set from typical undergraduate biology study material with default settings, validating all phases execute and outputs are complete.

input.study_material: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose. The process occurs in two main stages: light-dependent reactions in the thylakoid membrane and light-independent reactions (Calvin cycle) in the stroma. Light-dependent reactions produce ATP and NADPH, which power the Calvin cycle. The Calvin cycle uses these energy carriers to fix carbon dioxide into glucose. Chlorophyll absorbs photons and transfers electrons through the electron transport chain. Photosystem II absorbs light at 680 nm, while Photosystem I absorbs light at 700 nm. The Z-scheme describes electron flow between these photosystems.

test_cases.expected_behavior: All six phases execute successfully. Outputs include complete flashcard set with recall, elaboration, and example cards; learning sequence respecting prerequisites; spaced repetition schedule across 5 review days; quality report showing ≥85% coverage; deck metadata with realistic study time (3-8 hours for medium deck).

test_cases.name: happy_path_small_spanish_vocabulary
test_cases.scenario: Generate small flashcard deck (20-40 cards) from Spanish vocabulary study material with elaboration and examples enabled, validating appropriate deck size and concept granularity.

input.study_material: Los verbos regulares en español se conjugan siguiendo patrones predecibles. El verbo hablar (to speak) es un verbo regular de la primera conjugación (-ar). El verbo comer (to eat) es un verbo regular de la segunda conjugación (-er). El verbo vivir (to live) es un verbo regular de la tercera conjugación (-ir). La conjugación presente del verbo hablar es: yo hablo, tú hablas, él habla, nosotros hablamos, vosotros habláis, ellos hablan. Los verbos irregulares no siguen estos patrones, como ser (to be), estar (to be located), ir (to go), y tener (to have).

test_cases.expected_behavior: Deck size is 20-40 cards as specified. Concept granularity is broader (e.g., verb conjugations grouped). Learning sequence uses blocked interleaving (grouped by topic). Study load is 15-25 cards/day. First session completes in <90 minutes.

test_cases.name: edge_case_minimal_study_material
test_cases.scenario: Process minimal but valid study material (exactly 100 characters) with single concept, validating graceful handling of edge case input size.

input.study_material: Osmosis is the movement of water molecules across a semipermeable membrane from an area of higher water potential to lower water potential.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill handles minimal material gracefully. Extracts at least 1 concept. Generates base card + example card(s). Quality report shows coverage (may be <85% due to minimal material, but flags this appropriately). No errors or crashes.

test_cases.name: edge_case_large_deck_with_splitting
test_cases.scenario: Generate large flashcard deck (>150 cards) from comprehensive study material, validating deck splitting into sub-decks (Fundamentals, Intermediate, Advanced) and proper progression order.

input.study_material: Cellular respiration is the process by which cells break down glucose to produce ATP. Glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm and produces 2 ATP and 2 NADH per glucose. The citric acid cycle (Krebs cycle) occurs in the mitochondrial matrix and produces 2 ATP, 6 NADH, and 2 FADH2 per glucose. The electron transport chain occurs in the inner mitochondrial membrane and produces approximately 32-34 ATP per glucose through oxidative phosphorylation. Anaerobic respiration (fermentation) produces only 2 ATP per glucose and regenerates NAD+ for continued glycolysis. Lactic acid fermentation occurs in muscles during intense exercise. Alcoholic fermentation occurs in yeast and bacteria. Aerobic respiration is much more efficient than anaerobic respiration. The proton gradient across the inner mitochondrial membrane drives ATP synthase. Chemiosmosis is the mechanism by which ATP is produced. Substrate-level phosphorylation directly produces ATP in glycolysis and the citric acid cycle. Oxidative phosphorylation produces most ATP through the electron transport chain. Mitochondrial cristae increase surface area for the electron transport chain. The P/O ratio describes ATP produced per electron pair. Uncoupling proteins dissipate the proton gradient as heat. Brown adipose tissue uses uncoupling for thermogenesis. Metabolic rate varies with activity level and body composition. Basal metabolic rate is the energy required at rest. Caloric restriction affects metabolic efficiency.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Deck is split into 3 sub-decks with 50-100 cards each. Sub-decks are named Fundamentals, Intermediate, Advanced. Progression order is defined. Total cards exceed 150. Difficulty distribution spans all 5 levels. Estimated study time is 8-20 hours. No circular dependencies between sub-decks.

test_cases.name: error_handling_circular_prerequisite_dependency
test_cases.scenario: Process study material where concept prerequisites form a circular dependency (A requires B, B requires A), triggering error handling to identify bootstrap concepts and flag for user review.

input.study_material: Recursion is a programming technique where a function calls itself. Base cases are necessary to prevent infinite recursion. Recursive functions require understanding of function calls and stack frames. Stack frames are memory structures created when functions call themselves recursively. Understanding recursion requires understanding stack frames and function calls.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects circular dependency between concepts. Outputs error message identifying the circular relationship (e.g., 'Circular dependency detected: recursion ↔ stack frames'). Recommends bootstrap concept to break cycle. Continues execution with adjusted prerequisite graph. Quality report flags the issue for user review.

test_cases.name: error_handling_low_coverage_missing_concepts
test_cases.scenario: Process study material where extracted concepts cover <70% of major topics, triggering error handling to flag missing concepts and request supplementary material.

input.study_material: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy. It occurs in the chloroplast. Chlorophyll is the pigment that absorbs light. The process produces glucose and oxygen.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates coverage <85%. Outputs gap_analysis identifying missing concepts (e.g., light-dependent reactions, Calvin cycle, electron transport chain). Flags as high-priority improvement. Suggests requesting supplementary material or manual concept specification. Continues with partial deck.

test_cases.name: error_handling_study_load_exceeds_capacity
test_cases.scenario: Generate flashcard set where recommended daily study load exceeds 30 cards/day, triggering error handling to extend schedule and recalculate optimal load.

input.study_material: Anatomy studies the structure of the human body. The skeletal system includes 206 bones in adults. The axial skeleton includes the skull, vertebral column, and rib cage. The appendicular skeleton includes limbs and girdles. Bones are connected by ligaments. Cartilage provides cushioning. The muscular system includes over 600 muscles. Muscles are attached to bones by tendons. Muscles work in pairs: agonists and antagonists. The nervous system includes the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves. The cardiovascular system includes the heart, arteries, veins, and capillaries. The respiratory system includes lungs, trachea, and diaphragm. The digestive system includes mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine. The endocrine system produces hormones. The immune system protects against pathogens. The urinary system filters waste. The reproductive system enables reproduction.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates study load >30 cards/day for 14-day cycle. Outputs study_load_validation with is_optimal=false. Extends schedule to 21 or 30 days. Recalculates recommended_daily_cards to 15-25 range. Outputs note explaining adjustment. Spaced repetition schedule reflects extended cycle.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_prerequisite_constraints_satisfied
test_cases.scenario: Validate that learning sequence respects all prerequisite constraints and passes topological sort, ensuring no concept is presented before its prerequisites.

input.study_material: Algebra is the branch of mathematics that uses variables and equations. Variables represent unknown quantities. Equations are statements that two expressions are equal. Linear equations have variables raised to the first power. Quadratic equations have variables raised to the second power. Solving linear equations involves isolating the variable. Solving quadratic equations requires understanding factoring or the quadratic formula. Factoring is the process of breaking expressions into products. The quadratic formula is x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a. Polynomials are expressions with multiple terms. Degree of a polynomial is the highest power of the variable.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Learning sequence is generated with all prerequisite constraints satisfied. For every card in sequence, all prerequisite cards appear earlier. Topological sort validation passes. sequence_validation object shows prerequisites_satisfied=true and no_circular_dependencies=true. No concept appears before its prerequisites.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_alternation_pattern_base_example_elaboration
test_cases.scenario: Validate that learning sequence alternates between base, example, and elaboration cards following the pattern base → example → base → example, with at least 60% of transitions following this pattern.

input.study_material: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy in glucose. Light-dependent reactions occur in the thylakoid membrane. Light-independent reactions (Calvin cycle) occur in the stroma. Photosystem II absorbs light at 680 nm. Photosystem I absorbs light at 700 nm. The electron transport chain transfers electrons between photosystems. ATP synthase produces ATP from ADP and phosphate. NADPH is produced by Photosystem I. The Calvin cycle fixes carbon dioxide into glucose using ATP and NADPH. Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate (RuBP) is the 5-carbon sugar that combines with CO2. 3-phosphoglycerate is the 3-carbon product of carbon fixation.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Learning sequence shows alternation between card types. At least 60% of consecutive card transitions follow base → example → base pattern (or base → elaboration → base if no examples). sequence_validation shows alternation_pattern_applied=true. Cognitive variety is maintained throughout sequence.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_mastery_threshold_consistency
test_cases.scenario: Validate that mastery thresholds are assigned consistently: difficulty 1-2 cards require 2 correct recalls, difficulty 3 requires 3, difficulty 4-5 require 4, and elaboration cards require max(related cards) + 1.

input.study_material: Mitochondria are cellular organelles that produce ATP through cellular respiration. The inner mitochondrial membrane contains the electron transport chain. The outer mitochondrial membrane is permeable to small molecules. The cristae are folds of the inner membrane that increase surface area. The matrix is the innermost compartment containing enzymes. Glycolysis produces 2 ATP and 2 NADH per glucose molecule. The citric acid cycle produces 2 ATP, 6 NADH, and 2 FADH2 per glucose. The electron transport chain produces approximately 32-34 ATP per glucose through chemiosmosis. Oxidative phosphorylation couples electron transport to ATP synthesis. The proton gradient drives ATP synthase rotation.

test_cases.expected_behavior: All flashcards have mastery_threshold assigned. Thresholds follow heuristic: difficulty 1-2 → 2 correct, difficulty 3 → 3 correct, difficulty 4-5 → 4 correct. Elaboration cards have threshold = max(related base cards) + 1. quality_report.mastery_criteria shows all_assigned=true. No thresholds are <2 or >5.
